papers.title_en: Set-Based Transformer for Atmospheric Compensation in Standoff LWIR Hyperspectral Imaging
papers.authors: Fabian Perez, Nicolas Quintero, Jeferson Acevedo, Hoover Rueda-Chacon
papers.evidence: 深度学习用于大气补偿
papers.tldr: 被动长波红外高光谱成像在远距离探测中受大气吸收和发射影响，大气补偿因建模困难而被忽视。本文提出轻量级基于集合的Transformer框架，以不同距离的辐射测量为输入，联合估计透射率、大气路径辐射和共享下向辐射。实验表明，该方法在MODTRAN生成的模拟数据集上实现了低光谱畸变，且稀疏自编码器的潜特征可激活地理一致子集。该工作公开了代码和数据集，推动了大气补偿的实际应用。
papers.abstract_en: Passive long-wave infrared (LWIR) hyperspectral imaging under a standoff geometry depends on atmospheric absorption and emission, as well as reflected radiance, thus making atmospheric compensation essential to get knowledge of a target of interest. Despite its importance, this compensation has been largely overlooked due to its practical and modeling difficulty. In this paper, we present a lightweight set-based deep learning framework that takes multiple radiance measurements, collected at different standoff ranges, as input and jointly estimates transmittance, atmospheric path radiance, and a shared downwelling spectrum. We analyze the learned representation with a sparse autoencoder and observe that several latent features do activate on geographically coherent subsets of the test data despite the absence of location supervision. Experiments on a MODTRAN generated standoff LWIR dataset demonstrate low spectral distortion across all estimated products. The dataset and code is publicly available at: https://factral.co/SAE-LWIR/
